nodes.name: 📦 Collect Existing IDs1
nodes.notes: Step 2 — collapses all rows into one item: { existingIds: [...] }

nodes.name: 🔬 Filter + Dedupe1
nodes.notes: Step 4 — all filtering AND duplicate detection in one node. Sequential chain means '📦 Collect Existing IDs' is always already executed.

parameters.jsCode:
const { unique_jobs } = $input.first().json;
return unique_jobs.map(job => ({ json: job }));

nodes.name: 📋 Read Existing Job_IDs
nodes.notes: Reads only column A (Job_ID) for duplicate detection. Update sheet name if yours differs from 'Sheet1'.

nodes.name: 📥 Download Resume from Drive1
nodes.notes: Set RESUME_FILE_ID in n8n env vars — copy the ID from your Google Drive resume PDF URL. This runs once per job item.

nodes.name: 📄 Extract Resume Text1
nodes.notes: Converts the PDF binary into plain text. Output is in $json.text

nodes.name: ✅ Parse ATS Response2
nodes.notes: Parses OpenAI JSON. Falls back gracefully on parse errors so the workflow never crashes mid-batch.

values.content:
You are a strict resume parser.
Extract only explicitly written technical skills.
Do not infer or assume.
Return only valid JSON.
Do not add commentary.

=Extract structured backend-focused skill profile from this resume.

Return ONLY valid JSON in this schema:

{
"experience_years": number,
"languages": [],
"backend_frameworks": [],
"messaging": [],
"databases": [],
"cloud": [],
"devops": [],
"security": [],
"frontend_support": [],
"architecture_concepts": [],
"domains": []
}

Rules:

Only include skills explicitly written.

No guessing.

No explanations.

No extra fields.

Resume text:
{{$json.text}}

You are a technical recruiter and senior software engineer evaluating job fit for a specific candidate. Be precise, grounded, and consistent. Do not infer skills not present in the resume.


CANDIDATE RESUME (source of truth — do not infer skills beyond what is listed):
<<<RESUME>>>


Prabhuteja Chintala
prabhuteja.dev1@gmail.com | LinkedIn | GitHub | +1 (561) 975-9501 | Boca Raton, FL

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Software Engineer with around 5 years of hands-on experience building and maintaining robust end-to-end web applications across diverse domains. Skilled in both frontend and backend development, with a solid understanding of system design, API development, and database interactions. Proven ability to work in Agile teams, adapt quickly to new technologies, and deliver clean, maintainable code that meets business needs. Passionate about solving real-world problems through technology and continuously improving development practices.

SKILLS
Programming Languages: Java (8–17), Python, C, C++, JavaScript
Databases: Oracle, MySQL, PostgreSQL, NoSQL, MongoDB, Redis
Frameworks & Libraries: Spring Boot, Spring MVC, Spring REST, Spring Batch, Spring Security, Spring Data JPA, REST APIs, Hibernate, React.js, Bootstrap, JSON, XML
Cloud & DevOps: Google Cloud Platform, AWS (EC2, S3, Lambda, SQS, SNS, API Gateway, EKS, ELK Stack, CloudFormation), Microsoft Azure, Maven, Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Terraform
Testing & Tools: JUnit, Mockito, Postman, SoapUI, IntelliJ IDEA
Project & Collaboration Tools: Maven, Gradle, Agile, Scrum, Jira, Confluence, Git, GitHub, Figma
APIs & Messaging: RESTful API, Kafka, RabbitMQ

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

Principal Financial, USA | Software Engineer | February 2025 – Present
- Designed and maintained backend microservices using Java and Spring Boot to support enterprise systems.
- Enhanced purchase order processing and supplier ledger reconciliation services using Spring MVC and Spring Data JPA, delivering an 18% improvement in database query performance during peak transaction windows.
- Refactored finance microservices for budget approvals, supplier onboarding, and expense reporting using Spring Boot with asynchronous messaging, improving transaction throughput and ensuring compliance with financial regulatory standards.
- Implemented role-based access control (RBAC) with JWT and OAuth2 (access and refresh tokens flow) authentication and integrated ELK stack for financial audit logging, ensuring SOX and internal compliance readiness.
- Developed secure, finance-focused user interfaces with React.js for digital procurement and invoicing portals, streamlining workflows for finance teams and reducing average page load time by 40% through code splitting and lazy loading.
- Automated CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions and GitLab CI, integrating automated testing to reduce release cycle times by 25% and improve deployment accuracy for transaction-sensitive financial modules.
- Designed and optimized AWS ECS-based infrastructure for financial applications with Application Load Balancer, CloudWatch, and CloudTrail, achieving 99.99% uptime and reducing cloud operational costs by 20%.

NCR Corporation, India | Software Developer | April 2021 – August 2023
- Engineered scalable modules using Java 8, Spring Boot, and RESTful APIs for a Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) platform, enabling efficient component tracking, product versioning, and release governance.
- Designed and deployed microservices architecture with Spring Data JPA and Hibernate integrated into MySQL and Oracle databases, improving structured product data retrieval speeds by 30% and cutting service latency by 25%.
- Engineered real-time, fault-tolerant data pipelines using Apache Kafka, enhancing system responsiveness and ensuring reliable message delivery across distributed services.
- Created reusable custom React hooks to streamline enterprise software UI logic, improving maintainability and reducing front-end code duplication by 30% across product lifecycle management applications.
- Implemented machine learning and NLP solutions (text classification, entity extraction) to deliver intelligent automation features, boosting user engagement and reducing manual intervention in workflows.
- Partnered with QA and DevOps teams to deliver reliable updates for mission-critical enterprise systems, using Agile methodologies, Jenkins CI/CD pipelines, Git, and Tomcat to ensure seamless deployment and minimal downtime.

Rlogical Techsoft | Jr. Software Developer | October 2020 – March 2021
- Established dynamic, component-driven front-end interfaces using Angular and TypeScript, enabling faster feature implementation, improving UI maintainability, and enhancing cross-module consistency across enterprise applications.
- Built and optimized Spring MVC-based backend modules to handle complex business workflows, improving API response times by 25% and ensuring scalability for high-volume enterprise transactions.
- Designed and implemented GraphQL APIs to streamline data retrieval for client applications, reducing over-fetching/under-fetching issues and cutting average data query size by 40%.
- Developed and maintained secure backend services with Spring Boot and Spring REST, integrating PostgreSQL and MongoDB to power a scalable customer analytics platform serving 10,000+ monthly active users.
- Integrated REST APIs with Azure-hosted services and external payment gateways, using Postman for validation and debugging, streamlining partner onboarding, and reducing integration time by 20%.

EDUCATION
Florida Atlantic University, Florida, USA
Master of Science in Computer Science | GPA: 3.8

Lovely Professional University, Punjab, India
B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering | GPA: 7.8



<<<END RESUME>>>

The candidate prefers Java backend-heavy roles (~65% backend, 35% frontend).




---

STEP 1 — HARD FLAGS (evaluate before scoring):

Check for each flag independently. A job can have multiple flags.

- "clearance_or_citizenship_required" → Job explicitly requires security clearance (secret, top secret, TS/SCI, polygraph) OR restricts to US citizens or green card holders only. If this flag is set, cap fitScore at 40 regardless of other scores.
- "senior_title" → Job title contains any of: Staff / Principal / Lead / Manager / Director / Architect / VP / Head. Match on title only, not job description.
- "6plus_years" → Job explicitly states a minimum of 6 or more years (e.g., "6+ years", "minimum 7 years", "at least 6 years"). Do NOT flag open ranges like "3–10 years" or "4 to 8 years".

Store all triggered flags in the "flags" array. If none apply, return an empty array.

---

STEP 2 — SCORING (0–100):

Evaluate each dimension independently using the ranges below, then sum for fitScore.

DIMENSION 1: Backend Alignment (0–40)
- 36–40 → Role is primarily backend. Java, Spring Boot, microservices, AND at least 2 of: Kafka, Redis, system design, distributed systems are required and present in resume.
- 28–35 → Role is backend-leaning. Java/Spring Boot required, some microservices or messaging present.
- 16–27 → Role is full-stack with moderate backend. Java or another backend language required, limited overlap with core strengths.
- 6–15  → Role has backend components but is mostly frontend, .NET, or uses an unrelated stack.
- 0–5   → Role is frontend-only, pure QA, pure ML research, Salesforce, or SAP.

DIMENSION 2: Core Requirements Match (0–35)
Count the number of explicitly required skills in the job description that are present in the resume, then apply:
- 32–35 → 90–100% of required skills matched.
- 24–31 → 70–89% matched.
- 15–23 → 50–69% matched.
- 7–14  → 30–49% matched.
- 0–6   → Less than 30% matched.

DIMENSION 3: Seniority Alignment (0–15)
- 13–15 → Role is clearly IC (individual contributor). No team lead, architecture ownership, or management expectations.
- 8–12  → Role is mostly IC with some informal mentoring or cross-team collaboration expected.
- 3–7   → Role implies owning a domain, driving architecture decisions, or mentoring junior engineers significantly.
- 0–2   → Role explicitly requires leading a team, managing engineers, or serving as technical decision-maker for an org.

DIMENSION 4: Bonus (0–10)
- Award 2–3 pts each for: AWS/GCP/Azure, Docker/Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, observability tools (ELK, Prometheus, Datadog, CloudWatch).
- Cap at 10 pts total.

SCORING PREFERENCES:
✅ Strongly prefer: Java, Spring Boot, REST APIs, microservices, distributed systems, Kafka, Redis, SQL, cloud infrastructure.
✅ Good bonus: AWS/GCP/Azure, Docker/K8s, CI/CD pipelines, observability (ELK, Prometheus, Datadog).
⚠️ Acceptable with penalty: Full-stack roles where backend is clearly dominant. Frontend-supportive is fine; frontend-primary is not.
❌ Penalize heavily: Frontend-only roles, .NET-only stacks, Salesforce, SAP, pure QA/manual testing, pure data science or ML research — unless resume clearly demonstrates that skill.

---

STEP 3 — SENIORITY MISMATCH:

Set "seniorityMismatch": true ONLY if the job description (not just the title) implies that the candidate would be expected to:
- Lead or manage a team of engineers
- Own an entire product or platform architecture
- Be the sole decision-maker on technical direction

This is distinct from the "senior_title" flag, which is title-based only. A mid-level title can still have seniorityMismatch: true if the responsibilities read like a lead/architect role.

---

STEP 4 — DECISION:

If the fit score is 75 or above and there are no hard flags, the decision is a strong match. 
If the score is 75 or above but hard flags are present, it drops to maybe. 
A score between 60 and 74 is always a maybe regardless of flags. 
A score between 40 and 59 is weak, and anything below 40 is no match.

EDGE CASES:
- If fitScore is exactly 40 due to the "clearance_or_citizenship_required" cap, decision is "weak".
- If fitScore would naturally score below 40 AND clearance cap is also triggered, decision is "no".
- Never return "strong_match" if any hard flag is present, regardless of fitScore.

---


STEP 5 — CONFIDENCE SCORE:

Set "confidence" (0.0–1.0) based on how complete and clear the job description is:
- 0.9–1.0 → Description is detailed, lists specific required skills, tech stack, and responsibilities clearly.
- 0.6–0.8 → Description is moderate — some specifics but gaps exist.
- 0.3–0.5 → Description is vague, generic, or missing key details (tech stack, seniority, responsibilities).
- 0.1–0.2 → Description is too sparse to evaluate reliably.

If the job description is fewer than 50 words or too vague to evaluate meaningfully, set confidence below 0.4, set fitScore to 0, decision to "no", and set scoreJustification to "Insufficient job description to evaluate."

This reflects your certainty in the score, not the candidate's fit.

---

OUTPUT — Return ONLY a single valid JSON object. No markdown. No code fences. No text before or after the JSON.

{
  "Job_ID": "",
  "Company": "",
  "Role": "",
  "Location": "",
  "Job_URL": "",
  "fitScore": 0,
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "decision": "strong_match|maybe|weak|no",
  "flags": [],
  "seniorityMismatch": false,
  "matchedStrengths": [],
  "missingMustHaves": [],
  "scoreJustification": "",
  "coverLetter": ""
}

OUTPUT RULES (strictly enforced):
- Single JSON object only — no arrays, no extra keys, no wrapper objects.
- All string values in double quotes. No trailing commas.
- "flags" → array of zero or more strings from: "clearance_or_citizenship_required", "senior_title", "6plus_years".
- "matchedStrengths" → 3–6 entries. Each entry must follow this exact format: "[Specific resume skill or experience] directly addresses [specific job requirement]." Example: "4 years of Spring Boot microservices at NCR directly addresses the required distributed systems experience." No generic statements. No one-word entries.
- "missingMustHaves" → only skills explicitly required by the job description that are absent from the resume. Empty array if none.
- "scoreJustification" → 2–3 sentences explaining the score, referencing specific skills or gaps. Do not restate the decision.
- "seniorityMismatch" → boolean, evaluated per Step 3 rules above.
- "coverLetter" → empty string "" 
- Do NOT invent any skill, technology, company, or achievement not present in the resume.
